Context: Need PostgreSQL database with RLS and auto-generated APIs
Options Considered: 1. Supabase (PostgreSQL with RLS), 2. Google Cloud SQL, 3. Firebase Realtime DB, 4. MongoDB Atlas
Outcome: Selected Supabase for RLS, real-time features, and auto-generated APIs. Cost-effective and developer-friendly.

Decision: Use React DnD for page builder
Context: Need drag-and-drop functionality for page builder
Options Considered: 1. React DnD, 2. dnd-kit, 3. React Beautiful DnD, 4. Custom implementation
Outcome: Selected React DnD because it's already installed and has extensive documentation. Proven in production apps.

Options Considered: 1. 6-week aggressive timeline, 2. 12-week balanced, 3. 16-week comprehensive
Outcome: Selected 12 weeks to allow for proper testing and polish while maintaining momentum.

Context: Need safe testing environment before production changes
Options Considered: 1. No staging (prod only), 2. Staging + prod, 3. Dev + staging + prod
Outcome: Selected staging + prod as minimum viable multi-env. Dev can be local.

Options Considered: 1. WYSIWYG rich text editor, 2. Block-based builder, 3. Template-based, 4. Code-based
Outcome: Selected block-based for flexibility, structure, and ease of use. Similar to WordPress Gutenberg, Notion.

Options Considered: 1. JSON in database, 2. HTML storage, 3. Markdown, 4. Custom binary format
Outcome: Selected JSON for flexibility, version control, and easy inspection/debugging.

Options Considered: 1. Zod, 2. Yup, 3. Joi, 4. class-validator
Outcome: Selected Zod for TypeScript-first design and schema reuse between frontend/backend.

Options Considered: No alternatives considered - already installed and working
Outcome: Continue using existing component library. 50+ components already available.

Context: Need scalable file storage for media library
Options Considered: 1. Google Cloud Storage, 2. Supabase Storage, 3. AWS S3, 4. Cloudinary
Outcome: Pending decision. GCS preferred for GCP integration, but Supabase Storage simpler.

Context: How many users for initial testing
Options Considered: 1. 3 testers, 2. 5 testers, 3. 10 testers, 4. 20 testers
Outcome: Pending. Recommend 3-5 for MVP. Enough for feedback, small enough to manage.

Context: Should page builder work on mobile?
Options Considered: 1. Desktop-only for MVP, 2. Fully responsive, 3. Tablet-optimized
Outcome: Pending. Recommend desktop-only for MVP. Most CMS admin panels are desktop-centric.

Options Considered: 1. Cron job, 2. Cloud Scheduler, 3. Background worker, 4. Skip for MVP
Outcome: Pending. May defer to post-MVP if time constrained.

Context: Should we build plugin architecture in MVP?
Options Considered: 1. No plugins for MVP, 2. Basic plugin hooks, 3. Full plugin system
Outcome: Pending. Recommend defer to post-MVP. Focus on core features first.

Context: Support multiple organizations in one deployment?
Options Considered: 1. Single-tenant only, 2. Multi-tenant architecture
Outcome: Pending. Recommend single-tenant for MVP. Can refactor later if productizing.

Context: Should we add AI content generation (e.g., Claude API)?
Options Considered: 1. No AI for MVP, 2. AI block suggestions, 3. AI content generation
Outcome: Pending. Defer to post-MVP. Nice-to-have but not critical.

Options Considered: 1. Full snapshots per version, 2. Diff-based storage, 3. No versioning
Outcome: Selected full snapshots. Simpler implementation, acceptable storage cost for MVP.

Options Considered: No alternatives - IAP is already implemented
Outcome: Continue using Google Workspace SSO via IAP. Works well.

Options Considered: No alternatives - already established pattern
Outcome: Continue with TypeScript for type safety and better developer experience.

Options Considered: No alternatives - already established pattern
Outcome: Continue with Tailwind CSS. Team familiar, well-integrated.

Options Considered: 1. Keep monorepo, 2. Split into separate repos
Outcome: Keep monorepo for MVP. Simpler deployment and coordination.
